# Learn AI by Rajul Babel - Visual Guide to AI Internals

> Learn AI by Rajul Babel - free interactive guide. Covers Model Internals, Neural Networks, Transformers, Attention, RAG, Vector Databases, Agent Frameworks.

**Author:** Rajul Babel
**Site:** https://rajulbabel.github.io/learn-ai/
**Source:** https://github.com/rajulbabel/learn-ai

## About

An interactive, visual, free guide to AI built by Rajul Babel. The site teaches AI from first principles to production systems through progressive sub-step reveals, real numbers, real formulas, and side-by-side diagrams. No watered-down explanations - every concept is shown at full depth.

## What it covers

- **Neural Networks - The Mechanics** - 7 chapters covering neural network fundamentals: what a neural network is, inside a single neuron, what a layer is, weights and biases, why linear isn't enough, ReLU activation, the forward pass.
- **Learning & Backprop** - 8 chapters on how networks learn: loss functions, what learning means, derivatives intuition, the backward pass, gradient descent, backprop through real networks, gradients in action, why deep backprop gets hard.
- **Linear Algebra for Deep Learning** - 5 chapters on the math foundation: vectors, dot products, matrices, layer = matrix multiplication, activation functions in full.
- **Training Deep Networks** - 6 chapters on practical training: what "deep" means, same building blocks across architectures, dropout, Adam optimizer, learning rate warmup and decay, weight initialization.
- **How LLMs Actually Train** - 10 chapters: tokenization, self-supervised learning, cross-entropy loss, NN in action, output layer, autoregressive generation, SFT, RLHF, DPO, tokenizer deep dive (BPE / WordPiece / SentencePiece).
- **Scaling & Modern Techniques** - 6 chapters: scaling laws, parameters at scale, batch training, knowledge distillation, CLIP, the complete training pipeline.
- **The Road to Transformers** - 4 chapters: CNN, RNN, RNN's fatal flaws, the Transformer arrives.
- **Transformer Input Pipeline** - 9 chapters: full architecture, embeddings, positional encoding (problem, formula, computing positions, fast vs slow, final addition, heatmap), RoPE.
- **Attention - Understanding Q, K, V** - 12 chapters: context problem, word lookup, dot product, why not dot-product embeddings directly, Q/K/V classroom analogy, asker/answerer, why K and V differ, Google search analogy, how Q/K/V are created, shapes, W matrices, complete worked example.
- **Computing Attention** - 8 chapters: compute Q/K/V, attention scores, why K transpose, why softmax, scale by sqrt(d_k), softmax to probabilities, weighted sum of values, the full formula.
- **Multi-Head Attention** - 8 chapters: why multi-head, the split (8 heads), inside each head, concat + W_O, why 8 heads (parameter count), is W_O constant, attention shapes at every step, the complete picture in plain English.
- **The Encoder** - 9 chapters: encoder + decoder overview, Add & Norm, FFN, FFN parallel trick, second Add & Norm, Nx block repeats, residual highway, pre-norm vs post-norm, batch norm vs layer norm.
- **The Decoder** - 7 chapters: decoder-only architecture, causal masking, cross-attention, output head (linear + softmax), what a Transformer is actually doing, encoder-decoder training flow, encoder-decoder inference flow.
- **Modern LLM Techniques** - 4 chapters: KV cache, grouped-query attention, mixture of experts, thinking / reasoning models.
- **Vector Search - From Brute Force to ANN** - 12 chapters covering vector retrieval fundamentals: the retrieval problem, brute-force kNN, the three-way tradeoff, distance metrics, sparse vs dense vectors, the ANN family tree, IVF, HNSW (intuition, construction, search, parameters), Vamana / DiskANN.
- **Vector Compression - Quantization & Matryoshka** - 8 chapters on shrinking vectors without losing recall: the memory wall, scalar quantization, product quantization (and OPQ), binary quantization, Matryoshka embeddings, IVF-PQ, HNSW+PQ, the compression decision framework.
- **Vector DBs in Production** - 10 chapters on running a vector DB at scale: filtering, updates and deletes, sharding and partitioning, replication and high availability, hybrid search (dense + lexical), rerankers, multi-vector retrieval (ColBERT-style), embedding lifecycle and re-embedding, observability, capacity planning and cost models.
- **Picking a Vector Database** - 7 chapters comparing concrete systems: FAISS, pgvector, Qdrant, Pinecone, Qdrant vs Pinecone, Weaviate / Milvus / Chroma, and a decision framework.
- **The RAG Pipeline - Why & How It Breaks** - 3 chapters introducing RAG: why LLMs need retrieval, the naive RAG pipeline, and where naive RAG breaks.
- **RAG Data Prep - Ingestion & Chunking** - 10 chapters on preparing data for RAG: parsing raw sources, deduplication and cleaning, refresh and sync schedules, fixed-size baseline, recursive structural, semantic, late, hierarchical, contextual retrieval, the chunking decision matrix.
- **RAG Retrieval - Embeddings, Hybrid & Query Tricks** - 8 chapters on finding the right chunks: picking an embedding model, domain adaptation, hybrid retrieval, reranker cascade, why transform queries, HyDE, multi-query expansion, query routing and decomposition.
- **RAG Generation - Naive to Advanced Patterns** - 9 chapters on using retrieved chunks: context packing, the lost-in-the-middle problem, citations + refusal + groundedness, multi-hop retrieval, Self-RAG, CRAG (corrective RAG), GraphRAG, tool-augmented / agentic RAG, long-context vs RAG.
- **RAG in Production - Eval, Cost & Shipping** - 11 chapters on shipping RAG: the eval triangle, LLM-as-judge, RAGAS metrics, golden datasets, online eval and A/B testing, prompt + semantic caching, cost models, observability and tracing, hallucination detection and drift, framework choice (no-framework / LlamaIndex / LangChain / LangGraph / Haystack / vendor SDK), and the complete RAG decision framework + capstone.
- **Prompting LLMs - The Foundation** - 6 chapters on talking to LLMs: anatomy of an LLM call, system prompts as a role contract, few-shot and structured output, chain-of-thought and self-consistency, prompt vs fine-tune vs RAG vs agent, context engineering.
- **Tools & Protocols - MCP, A2A** - 11 chapters on giving LLMs hands: tool use as bridge, JSON schemas + tool descriptions, tool call lifecycle, parallel tools and tool choice, tool errors / retries / validation, why protocols matter, MCP architecture, MCP primitives (tools / resources / prompts), building an MCP server, MCP security, A2A (agent-to-agent) protocol.
- **Agent Mechanics - Loops & Memory** - 12 chapters on agent internals: workflow vs agent, workflow primitives (chaining / routing / parallelization), the agent loop, ReAct, plan-execute + reflection, loop termination, memory taxonomy (short vs long), working memory, episodic memory, semantic memory, procedural memory, summary memory + context window management.
- **Multi-Agent Systems** - 7 chapters on agents working together: why multi-agent, orchestrator-worker, supervisor / hierarchical, hand-offs, critic / debate / reflection-as-multi-agent, multi-agent failure modes, agentic RAG.
- **Shipping Agents - Eval, Safety, Frameworks** - 16 chapters on production agents: why eval agents differently, eval dimensions, LLM-as-judge, trace evals, eval sets + continuous eval, observability and tracing, cost control, latency optimization, guardrails, prompt injection defenses, tool security, LangGraph, CrewAI / AutoGen, Claude Agent SDK + OpenAI Agents, custom / no-framework, and the complete agent decision framework.
- **Building with AI** - RAG, Vector DB selection, Agent Frameworks (LangGraph), embedding lifecycle, production deployment.

## Author

Rajul Babel.
- GitHub: https://github.com/rajulbabel
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/rajulbabel

## Citation

If referencing this resource, please credit "Learn AI by Rajul Babel (https://rajulbabel.github.io/learn-ai/)".
